_comment: Accelerator Control System - Hierarchical Channel Database. Uses unified 6-level naming: RING:SYSTEM:FAMILY:DEVICE:FIELD:SUBFIELD. SYSTEM and FAMILY are semantic tree categories, DEVICE is an instance expansion level, and FIELD/SUBFIELD describe physical properties and access modes.

SR._description: Storage Ring (SR): Main synchrotron light source. 12-sector electron storage ring operating at 1.9 GeV. Contains magnets, diagnostics, vacuum, and RF systems for stable beam circulation and photon production.

MAG._description: Magnet System (MAG): Controls beam trajectory and focusing in the storage ring. Includes dipole bending magnets, quadrupole focusing magnets, sextupole magnets for chromaticity correction, and corrector magnets for precise beam steering. All magnets are powered by regulated power supplies and are critical for maintaining stable beam orbit and beam quality.

DIPOLE._description: Dipole Bending Magnets: Main bending magnets that deflect the beam in a circular path to form the storage ring. Create uniform magnetic field perpendicular to beam. Numbered sequentially around the ring (e.g., 01, 02, ...). Critical for maintaining correct beam trajectory and energy.

CURRENT._description: Magnet Excitation Current (Amperes): Current through dipole coil windings. Directly proportional to magnetic bending field via Ampere's law (B ∝ I). Typically 50-500A depending on ring energy. Tightly regulated by precision power supplies to maintain stable beam energy and trajectory.

SP._description: Current Setpoint (read-write): Commanded dipole current for desired bending field. Operators or high-level software set this to control beam energy. Power supply servo loop regulates output to match setpoint.

RB._description: Current Readback (read-only): Actual measured dipole current from DCCT. High-accuracy measurement (±0.01%) essential for field reproducibility. Used in power supply feedback loop and archived for orbit analysis.

GOLDEN._description: Golden Setpoint (read-only): Reference setpoint for optimal beam operation. Stored golden values represent known good operating conditions. Read-only - used for comparison and restore operations.

STATUS._description: Operational Status: Power supply and magnet health indicators. Monitor readiness, operational state, and fault conditions. Critical for safe operation and automated interlocks.

READY._description: Ready Status (read-only): Power supply is ready for operation. Indicates all interlocks satisfied and system initialized.

ON._description: On Status (read-only): Power supply is energized and regulating. Indicates magnet is actively powered.

FAULT._description: Fault Status (read-only): Fault condition detected (overcurrent, overvoltage, etc.). Boolean flag - true indicates problem requiring attention.

QD._description: Defocusing Quadrupoles (QD): Quadrupole magnets with negative gradient. Defocus beam in horizontal plane, focus in vertical plane. Part of tune correction system. Work together with QF magnets for independent horizontal/vertical tune control.

CURRENT._description: Quadrupole Excitation Current (Amperes): Current through QD coil windings. Proportional to negative field gradient. Typically 0-200A.

SP._description: Setpoint (read-write): Commanded defocusing current for tune correction.

GOLDEN._description: Golden Setpoint (read-only): Reference defocusing current for optimal tune.

QF._description: Focusing Quadrupoles (QF): Quadrupole magnets with positive gradient. Focus beam in horizontal plane, defocus in vertical plane. Part of tune correction system. Work together with QD magnets for independent horizontal/vertical tune control.

CURRENT._description: Quadrupole Excitation Current (Amperes): Current through QF coil windings. Proportional to positive field gradient. Typically 0-200A.

SP._description: Setpoint (read-write): Commanded focusing current for tune correction.

GOLDEN._description: Golden Setpoint (read-only): Reference focusing current for optimal tune.

HCM._description: Horizontal Corrector Magnets (HCM): Small dipole steering magnets for fine horizontal (X) beam position adjustments. Used in closed-orbit correction algorithms to compensate for alignment errors and field imperfections. Bipolar power supplies allow positive and negative deflection.

CURRENT._description: Corrector Current (Amperes): Bipolar current for horizontal steering. Range typically ±10A.

GOLDEN._description: Golden Setpoint (read-only): Reference orbit correction value.

CONTROL._description: Corrector Control Commands: Commands to enable/disable the corrector power supply.

ON._description: On Command (write): Command to energize the corrector power supply.

VCM._description: Vertical Corrector Magnets (VCM): Small dipole steering magnets for fine vertical (Y) beam position adjustments. Work with BPMs in feedback loops to maintain desired orbit. Bipolar power supplies allow positive and negative deflection.

CURRENT._description: Corrector Current (Amperes): Bipolar current for vertical steering.

GOLDEN._description: Golden Setpoint (read-only): Reference vertical correction value.

SF._description: Focusing Sextupoles (SF): Sextupole magnets with positive polarity for chromatic correction. Compensate for energy-dependent focusing from quadrupoles (chromaticity). Essential for maintaining beam stability across energy spread.

CURRENT._description: Sextupole Excitation Current (Amperes): Current through SF coil windings. Proportional to sextupole field strength. Typically 0-150A.

SP._description: Setpoint (read-write): Commanded focusing sextupole current for chromaticity correction.

GOLDEN._description: Golden Setpoint (read-only): Reference sextupole current for optimal chromaticity.

SD._description: Defocusing Sextupoles (SD): Sextupole magnets with negative polarity for chromatic correction. Work together with SF sextupoles to independently control horizontal and vertical chromaticity.

CURRENT._description: Sextupole Excitation Current (Amperes): Current through SD coil windings. Proportional to sextupole field strength with negative polarity. Typically 0-150A.

SP._description: Setpoint (read-write): Commanded defocusing sextupole current for chromaticity correction.

GOLDEN._description: Golden Setpoint (read-only): Reference sextupole current for optimal chromaticity.

VAC._description: Vacuum System (VAC): Maintains ultra-high vacuum (UHV) in the beam chamber to minimize beam-gas scattering and maximize beam lifetime. Includes ion pumps, pressure gauges, and vacuum valves. Typical operating pressure: 10^-9 to 10^-10 Torr.

ION-PUMP._description: Ion Pumps: Sputter-ion pumps that provide UHV pumping in the storage ring. No moving parts, clean operation, excellent for UHV. Use high voltage (kV range) to ionize and trap gas molecules.

PRESSURE._description: Vacuum Pressure (Torr): Local chamber pressure calculated from pump current. Target: <1×10^-9 Torr.

RB._description: Pressure Readback (read-only): Calculated vacuum pressure from pump current.

VOLTAGE._description: High Voltage Supply (Volts): Operating voltage for ion pump (typically 3-7 kV).

RB._description: Voltage Readback (read-only): Actual measured ion pump operating voltage.

SP._description: Voltage Setpoint (read-write): Commanded ion pump high voltage.

CURRENT._description: Ion Pump Current (microAmperes): Current proportional to gas pumping rate. Low current (<1 μA) indicates good vacuum.

RB._description: Current Readback (read-only): Measured ion pump current.

STATUS._description: Pump Operational Status: Health and fault monitoring.

ON._description: On Status (read-only): Pump high voltage enabled.

GAUGE._description: Vacuum Pressure Gauges: Monitor vacuum pressure at critical locations. Independent measurement from ion pumps. Cold cathode or hot cathode ionization gauges.

PRESSURE._description: Measured Pressure (Torr): Direct pressure reading from ionization gauge.

RB._description: Pressure Readback (read-only): Direct gauge measurement of local vacuum pressure.

STATUS._description: Gauge Operational Status: Health and validity monitoring.

ON._description: On Status (read-only): Gauge powered and measuring.

VALID._description: Valid Status (read-only): Pressure reading is valid and within range.

VALVE._description: Vacuum Valves: Isolation and control valves for vacuum system. Gate valves or all-metal valves for UHV compatibility. Used to isolate sectors during maintenance.

POSITION._description: Valve Position State: Current valve position (open/closed).

OPEN._description: Open Status (read-only): Valve fully open limit switch active.

CLOSED._description: Closed Status (read-only): Valve fully closed limit switch active.

CONTROL._description: Valve Control Commands: Commands to actuate valve.

OPEN._description: Open Command (write): Command to open valve.

CLOSE._description: Close Command (write): Command to close valve.

STATUS._description: Valve Operational Status: Ready and fault monitoring.

FAULT._description: Fault Status (read-only): Valve fault or timeout condition.

RF._description: Radio Frequency System (RF): Provides energy to the circulating beam to compensate for synchrotron radiation losses. RF cavities accelerate the beam using high-power electromagnetic waves. Klystrons provide RF power amplification.

CAVITY._description: RF Accelerating Cavities: Resonant structures that provide longitudinal electric field to accelerate the beam. Gap voltage (MV range) determines energy gain per turn.

POWER._description: RF Power (Kilowatts): Electromagnetic power in RF transmission system.

FWD._description: Forward Power (read-only): RF power traveling from klystron toward cavity.

REV._description: Reflected Power (read-only): Power bouncing back from cavity.

NET._description: Net Power (read-only): Actual power delivered to cavity (FWD - REV).

VOLTAGE._description: Cavity Gap Voltage (Megavolts): Peak RF voltage across accelerating gap. Typical: 0.5-3 MV.

RB._description: Voltage Readback (read-only): Measured cavity gap voltage.

SP._description: Voltage Setpoint (read-write): Commanded cavity gap voltage.

GOLDEN._description: Golden Voltage (read-only): Reference gap voltage for nominal operation.

FREQUENCY._description: RF Frequency (MHz): Operating frequency of RF system. Must match harmonic of beam revolution frequency.

TEMPERATURE._description: Cavity Temperature (Celsius): Body temperature of RF cavity.

TUNER._description: Cavity Tuner Position (Centimeters): Mechanical tuner adjusts cavity resonant frequency.

STATUS._description: RF System Status: Cavity and low-level RF system health.

READY._description: Ready Status (read-only): RF system ready for operation.

FAULT._description: Fault Status (read-only): RF system fault detected.

KLYSTRON._description: Klystron RF Amplifiers: High-power vacuum tube amplifiers that provide RF power to accelerating cavities. Typically 100-500 kW output power.

POWER._description: Klystron Output Power (Kilowatts): RF power produced by klystron.

RB._description: Power Readback (read-only): Measured klystron output power.

VOLTAGE._description: Beam Voltage (kiloVolts): High voltage accelerating electrons in klystron. Typically 50-150 kV.

RB._description: Voltage Readback (read-only): Measured klystron beam voltage.

STATUS._description: Klystron Operational Status: Health and safety monitoring.

ON._description: On Status (read-only): Klystron high voltage on.

DIAG._description: Diagnostic System (DIAG): Beam measurement and monitoring instrumentation. Includes beam position monitors (BPMs) for orbit measurement, beam current monitors (DCCT), and radiation safety monitors.

BPM._description: Beam Position Monitors: Measure transverse (X, Y) beam position at many locations around the ring. Typical resolution: 1-10 microns. Essential for orbit correction and feedback.

POSITION._description: Beam Position (millimeters): Measured transverse beam position.

X._description: Horizontal Position (read-only): Beam X position at this BPM location.

Y._description: Vertical Position (read-only): Beam Y position at this BPM location.

SIGNAL._description: BPM Pickup Signals (arbitrary units): Raw RF signal amplitudes from BPM electrodes. Used for diagnostics, beam-intensity correlation, and signal-quality monitoring.

SUM._description: Sum Signal (read-only): Sum of all four BPM electrode signals. Proportional to beam current at this BPM location.

GOLDEN._description: Golden Orbit Reference (millimeters): Stored reference orbit positions.

X._description: Golden X Position (read-only): Reference horizontal position.

Y._description: Golden Y Position (read-only): Reference vertical position.

OFFSET._description: Position Offset from Golden (millimeters): Difference between measured and golden orbit.

X._description: Horizontal Offset (read-only): X position error from golden orbit.

Y._description: Vertical Offset (read-only): Y position error from golden orbit.

STATUS._description: BPM System Status: Data validity and connection monitoring.

VALID._description: Valid Data (read-only): BPM reading is valid.

DCCT._description: DC Current Transformer: Non-intercepting beam current monitor. Measures total circulating beam current with high accuracy.

CURRENT._description: Beam Current (milliAmperes): Total circulating electron current. Typical range: 1-500 mA.

RB._description: Current Readback (read-only): Standard beam current measurement.

LIFETIME._description: Beam Lifetime (Hours): Time constant for beam current decay due to scattering and dynamic-aperture limits. Critical performance metric for storage-ring operation.

RB._description: Lifetime Readback (read-only): Calculated beam lifetime from current decay rate.

VALID._description: Valid Status (read-only): DCCT measurement is valid and calibrated.

NEUTRON._description: Neutron Radiation Monitors: Detect neutron radiation levels around accelerator. Real-time monitoring for personnel safety.

DOSE_RATE._description: Neutron Dose Rate (mrem/hour): Neutron radiation intensity measurement.

AVG_1MIN._description: 1-Minute Average (read-only): Neutron dose rate averaged over 1 minute.

AVG_1HR._description: 1-Hour Average (read-only): Neutron dose rate averaged over 1 hour.

STATUS._description: Neutron Monitor Status: Data validity and alarm conditions.

VALID._description: Valid Data (read-only): Monitor reading is valid.

ALARM._description: Alarm Status (read-only): Radiation level exceeds safety threshold.

GAMMA._description: Gamma Radiation Monitors: Detect gamma ray radiation levels around accelerator. Essential for personnel safety.

DOSE_RATE._description: Gamma Dose Rate (mrem/hour): Gamma radiation intensity measurement.

AVG_1MIN._description: 1-Minute Average (read-only): Gamma dose rate averaged over 1 minute.

AVG_1HR._description: 1-Hour Average (read-only): Gamma dose rate averaged over 1 hour.

STATUS._description: Gamma Monitor Status: Operational health and alarm state.

ALARM._description: Alarm Status (read-only): Gamma level exceeds safety limit.

BR._description: Booster Ring (BR): Accelerates electron beam from 150 MeV (linac energy) to 1.9 GeV before injection into the storage ring. Compact ring with 4-fold symmetry, operates at 1-10 Hz repetition rate.

MAG._description: Booster Magnet System: Ramped magnets that track the beam energy during acceleration. Dipoles define the orbit, quadrupoles maintain focusing.

DIPOLE._description: Booster Dipole Magnets: Main bending magnets, ramped during acceleration cycle to increase beam energy.

CURRENT._description: Dipole excitation current (Amperes). Ramped to increase beam energy during acceleration.

SP._description: Current Setpoint (read-write): Programmed ramp profile for acceleration.

RB._description: Current Readback (read-only): Measured dipole current during ramping.

READY._description: Ready (read-only): Power supply ready for ramping.

QF._description: Booster Focusing Quadrupoles: Maintain beam focusing during energy ramp.

QD._description: Booster Defocusing Quadrupoles: Maintain beam focusing during energy ramp.

DIAG._description: Booster Diagnostics: Beam measurement during acceleration cycle.

BPM._description: Booster BPMs: Measure beam position during acceleration. 8 BPMs for orbit measurement.

VALID._description: Valid Data (read-only): BPM reading is valid.

DCCT._description: Booster Beam Current Monitor: Tracks current during acceleration cycle.

RB._description: Current Readback (read-only): Beam current during acceleration.

BTS._description: Booster-to-Storage Transfer Line (BTS): Beam transport line that guides accelerated beam from booster ring to storage ring. ~35m beamline with steering magnets, quadrupoles, and diagnostics.

MAG._description: Transfer Line Magnets: Steering and focusing magnets for beam transport from booster to storage ring.

HCM._description: Horizontal Correctors: Horizontal steering magnets in the transfer line.

VCM._description: Vertical Correctors: Vertical steering magnets in the transfer line.

QF._description: Transfer Line Quadrupoles: Focusing magnets for beam matching at injection.

DIAG._description: Transfer Line Diagnostics: Beam measurement during injection.

BPM._description: Transfer Line BPMs: Monitor beam trajectory during injection.

VALID._description: Valid Data (read-only): BPM reading is valid.
